title: Stored Procedures
content:
### Stored Procedures

A stored procedure is a prepared SQL code block that can be saved and reused. Procedures can accept parameters and return results.

---

### Stored Procedure Syntax

```sql
-- MySQL
CREATE PROCEDURE procedure_name (IN param1 INT)
BEGIN
    SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department_id = param1;
END;

-- PostgreSQL
CREATE PROCEDURE procedure_name(param1 INT)
LANGUAGE SQL
AS $$
    SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department_id = param1;
$$;
```

### Calling a Stored Procedure

```sql
CALL GetEmployeesByDepartment(5);

EXEC GetEmployeesByDepartment @dept_id = 5;
```

### Benefits of Stored Procedures

| Benefit | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Performance | Pre-compiled, cached execution plans |
| Security | Users can execute without table-level permissions |
| Reusability | Write once, call many times |
| Network traffic | Reduced data transfer vs sending large queries |
| Consistency | Business logic enforced at the database level |

### Stored Procedure with Parameters

```sql
-- MySQL
CREATE PROCEDURE GetEmployeeCount(IN dept_name VARCHAR(50), OUT count INT)
BEGIN
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO count
    FROM employees
    WHERE department = dept_name;
END;

CALL GetEmployeeCount('Engineering', @count);
SELECT @count;
```

### Dropping a Procedure

```sql
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS procedure_name;
```

> **Key Point:** Stored procedures centralize business logic in the database. They vary significantly in syntax between database systems.

quiz.question: What is a stored procedure?
options: A saved SQL code block that can be reused
